question: Recall the “air samples”  illustrative data presented earlier in this chapter. Table 4.2 lists data for particulate matter at two sampling sites.
answer:
For site 1, the ordered array or data is:

 egin{tabular}{cccccccc}
22 & 24 & & 28 & 32 & & 36 & 38 & & 42 & 68 \
& & $\uparrow$ & & & $\uparrow$ & & & $\uparrow$ & \
& & Q1 & & & M & & & $\mathrm{Q3}$ &
\end{tabular}

Thus, Q1 $=$ the average of 24 and 28 (which is 26 ) and Q3 $=$ the average of 38 and 42 (which is 40 ). The $\mathrm{IQR}=40-26=14$.

For site 2, the ordered array is:

 egin{tabular}{cccccccc}
32 & 33 & & 34 & 36 & & 36 & 38 & & 39 & 40 \
& & $\uparrow$ & & & $\uparrow$ & & & $\uparrow$ & \
& & Q1 & & & M & & & $\mathrm{Q3}$ &
\end{tabular}
$\mathrm{Q} 1=33.5$ and $\mathrm{Q} 3=38.5$. Therefore, $\mathrm{IQR}=38.5-33.5=5$.
Data are less variable at site 2 (IQR: 14 vs. 5).
